rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CRESTWOOD BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Crestwood Behavioral Health, Inc.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings (Duplicate Entry For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 17, 2019, Line 3, (part 2 of 2 for tentative ruling) PETITIONER CRESTWOOD BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings Because the parties' agreement to submit all of Plaintiff's claims to an arbitral forum is enforceable, the proceedings before the Labor Commissioner shall be stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings in order to avoid frustration of the main object of the parties' agreement: a quick and streamlined resolution of their disputes governed by the Policy. (See Preston v. Ferrer (2008) 552 U.S. 346, 357 [arbitration following the Labor Commissioner's decision "would likely be long delayed, in contravention of Congress' intent 'to move the parties to an arbitrable dispute out of court and into arbitration as quickly and easily as possible.'"]). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Crestwood is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CRESTWOOD BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 17, 2019, Line 3, (part 1 of 2 for tentative ruling) PETITIONER CRESTWOOD BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings Petitioners Crestwood Behavioral Health, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is granted and the DLSE proceedings shall be stayed pending the arbitration of plaintiff DeEveria Lacy's claims. This order shall constitute the Court's statement of decision on the petition pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. § 1291. There is no dispute that Plaintiff signed a Dispute Resolution & Arbitration Policy, which requires binding arbitration of all employment-related legal disputes. Contrary to Crestwood's argument, that Policy does not clearly and unmistakably delegate the threshold issues of arbitrability and enforceability to the arbitrator. The arbitration clause provides that the parties waive all rights to a civil court action for disputes covered by the policy, including without limitation disputes "arising out of or relating to the interpretation or application of this Policy." That clause does not mention the threshold issues of enforceability or validity. (Cf. Aanderud v. Superior Court (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 880, 892 [delegation clause by which the parties agreed to arbitrate "all disputes, claims and controversies arising out of or relating to . . . the interpretation, validity, or enforceability of this Agreement, including the determination of the scope or applicability of this Section 5 [the 'Arbitration of Disputes' section]" provided clear and unmistakable evidence that the parties intended to delegate threshold issue of arbitrability to the arbitrator]; compare also Malone v. Superior Court (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1551, 1560.]) Plaintiff's argument that defendant failed to provide her with a notice to arbitrate is meritless. Plaintiff, as the party asserting employment-related claims, was required by the Policy to make a written demand for arbitration. The California Arbitration Act expressly authorizes Crestwood to file a petition to compel arbitration. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1281.2.) While Plaintiff correctly asserts that the Labor Commissioner has jurisdiction over her claims, waiver of the right to a Berman hearing does not necessarily render an arbitration agreement unenforceable or unconscionable per se, so long as "the arbitral scheme at issue provides employees with an accessible and affordable process for resolving wage disputes." (Sonic-Calabasas A, Inc. v. Moreno (2013) 57 Cal.4th 1109, 1146-48.) Plaintiff has not submitted any evidence that the Policy is procedurally unconscionable. (See Performance Team Freight Systems, Inc. v. Aleman (2015) 241 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1247 ["Absent any evidence, we cannot just assume there was procedural unconscionability."].) Nor does she contend that the Policy is substantively unconscionable, or that the arbitral forum provided under the Policy is not accessible or affordable. (Cf. Subcontracting Concepts (CT), LLC v. De Melo (Cal. Ct. App. Apr. 10, 2019) 2019 WL 1552684.) (See part 2 of 2 for entire tentative ruling.) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SEIU-USWW, AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. PREFERRED BUILDING SERVICES, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Re Class Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 17, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFFs SEIU-USWW, AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION, and JUAN MARTINEZ's Motion Re Class Notice Hearing required with regard to Plaintiff's proposed form of class notice and the proposed schedule. Defendant Preferred Building Services, Inc. shall bear the cost of notice. It is appropriate to require Defendant to bear the cost of notice because it may do so readily and at modest expense, and because the Court has previously granted summary judgment in Plaintiffs' favor. (See Hypertouch, Inc. v. Superior Court (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 1527, 1551-54.) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD MIRANDA VS. 1883 14TH AVENUE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint To Substitute True Names For Fictitiously Named Cross-Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 17, 2019, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANT WILLIAM THOMSON's Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint To Substitute True Names For Fictitiously Named Cross-Defendants Defendant and Cross-Complainant William J. Thomson dba Thomson Inspection Service's motion for leave to amend cross-complaint to substitute true names for fictitiously named cross-defendants is granted. Section 474 is to be liberally construed, and "even though the plaintiff knows of the existence of the defendant sued by a fictitious name, and even though the plaintiff knows the defendant's actual identity (that is, his name), the plaintiff is 'ignorant' within the meaning of the statute if he lacks knowledge of that person's connection with the case or with his injuries." (See General Motors Corp. v. Superior Court (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 580, 593-94.) "Although it is true . . . that a plaintiff need not be aware of each and every detail concerning a person's involvement before the plaintiff loses his ignorance [citations], it is equally true that the plaintiff does not relinquish her rights under section 474 simply because she has a suspicion of wrongdoing arising from one or more facts she does know." (Id. at 594-95.) "The plaintiff's knowledge is tested at the time suit is filed, and there is no requirement that the plaintiff exercise reasonable diligence to discover the identity of the defendant after filing the complaint." (Sobeck & Associates v. B & R Investments No. 24 (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 861, 867.) Here, on May 23, 2016, when Thomson filed his cross-complaint, Thomson has shown that he was not aware of facts giving rise to causes of action for indemnity and contribution against Defendants Vanguard Properties, Inc., Rusty Wadatz and Colleen Cotter (the Vanguard Defendants). Moreover, the Vanguard Defendants have not shown any prejudice from Thomson's asserted delay in seeking to amend the cross-complaint, nor could they, as the case was stayed from October 4, 2016 until at least December 7, 2018, and is not set for trial until May 4, 2020. (Cf. A.N. v. County of Los Angeles (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1058, 1066-69 [trial judge reasonably concluded that Doe amendments filed on eve of trial, more than two years after complaint was filed, were prejudicial].) The remaining Defendants (1883 14th Avenue, LLC, Otiana Investments, LLC, Ravi Anand, Spilsted Design Associates, R&D Construction Co., Inc., David Cao, and Anand & Associates) have not filed opposition to the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Thomson is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PITTS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Charles Folger Notice Of Motion For Issue Sanction And Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 17 2019, Line 2, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Charles Folger's Motion For Issue Sanction And Monetary Sanction. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is unopposed and establishes that Plaintiff has engaged in a misuse of the discovery by failing to comply with the Court's December 21, 2018 order requiring Plaintiff to serve verified answers without objections to Defendants' Form Interrogatories (Set 1), Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents Set No. 1. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.030 (b), if Plaintiff fails comply fully with the Court's December 21, 2018 order by serving verified answers without objections to Defendants' Form Interrogatories (Set 1), Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents Set No. 1 on the City Attorney's Office by 5 p.m. on Wednesday May 1, 2019, the Court further orders as an issue sanction that Plaintiff will be prohibited from supporting any claim for false arrest and excessive force against Defendants. The motion for monetary sanctions is denied as plaintiff is a low income pro per litigant. This order is issued without prejudice to Defendants' right to seek a terminating sanction resulting in dismissal of the action pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.030 (d) if Plaintiff fails to serve the previously ordered verified answers without objections to Defendants' Form Interrogatories (Set 1), Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents Set No. 1 on the City Attorney's Office by 5 p.m. on Wednesday May 1, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to skmediate@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES LLP VS. LAMA ASHKAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 17, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES LLP's Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Plaintiff Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP's motion to set aside dismissal is denied without prejudice. There is no proof of service of the motion on Defendant Lama Ashkar. According to the declaration of George King, he no longer represents Ashkar, and advised Plaintiff's counsel to "deal directly with defendant if she wants to pursue this motion." In addition, Plaintiff asserts that setting aside the dismissal would allow Plaintiff to file a motion to enforce the settlement agreement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6. However, Plaintiff has not shown that the parties requested the Court to retain jurisdiction over the parties to enforce the settlement prior to the Court's November 20, 2018 order dismissing the case. If the parties did not make such a timely request, the Court would not have jurisdiction to entertain a motion under section 664.6. (See, e.g., Sayta v. Chu (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 960, 962.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY DONG VS. KIMCO REALTY CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 17, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANTs KIMKO REALTY CORPORATION, 280 METRO, LP, 280 METRO HC, LLC, MARIA TEOS, and RANFIE ANCELOVICI's Motion For Relief From Default Defendants Kimco Realty Corporation, 280 Metro LP, 280 Metro HC, LLC, Maria Teos and Ranfie Ancelovici's motion to vacate the defaults entered on January 22, 2019 and January 25, 2019 is granted. Defendants have shown good cause for relief under the discretionary provision of CCP § 473(b). Defendants filed this motion within a reasonable time after entry of the defaults and have shown they were entered as the result of mistake, inadvertence, and/or excusable neglect. The declarations of Kimberley Hill and Glenn Brettschneider show that Ms. Hill's sickness and miscommunication with Mr. Brettschneider caused the defaults to be entered. In addition, the Court disapproves plaintiff's counsel's attempt to "quick pitch" the defaults past defendants after he had been negotiating with their adjuster and counsel. "[T]he policy of the law is to have every litigated case tried upon its merits, and it looks with disfavor upon a party, who, regardless of the merits of the case, attempts to take advantage of the mistake, surprise, inadvertence, or neglect of his adversary." (Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 855; see also Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 701 & n.10.) Defendants acted diligently to set aside the defaults. Plaintiff's request for fees is denied. Defendants have until April 22, 2019 to file their proposed pleadings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SMAATO, INC. VS. SABIO MOBILE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order
